stem: What does a computer fundamentally do?

options.text: It takes instructions, processes information, and produces results
It can think and feel like a human
It only stores files and photos

explanation_after: A computer is an instruction-following machine. It processes inputs into outputs — nothing more, nothing less. It does not think or feel.

stem: Which of the following is NOT a component of a computer?
explanation_after: Computers have CPU, RAM, and storage — all physical hardware. Imagination is a human quality. Computers only do what they are explicitly instructed to do.

stem: Why is the phrase 'computers are intelligent' misleading?

options.text: Because computers only follow exact instructions — they don't understand or reason
Because only robots can be intelligent

explanation_after: Computers execute instructions with no understanding. Even AI systems are sophisticated instruction-following — they don't 'understand' the way humans do. This distinction matters deeply when building AI systems.
